footnotes: a. Participants will be admitted to the CRU (clinical research unit) at least 10 hours prior to enrollment and initiation of copper_ and molybdenum_controlled diet.
b. At the CRU’s discretion, participants may remain in the CRU or be readmitted on Day 22 with all procedures starting on Day 23.
c. Unscheduled study visits may occur at any time during the study and may include any study procedure as deemed necessary by the Investigator.
d. Participants are required to return to the CRU Day 54+/_2; participants will be discontinued from ALXN1840 medication by their physician at the latest by the Day 54 follow_up.

e. Discharge from the unit may occur after completion of all procedures on Day 9 and on Day 40 after the Investigator has reviewed all safety assessment (including safety laboratory tests) and confirmed that the participant is appropriate for discharge.

f. A single outpatient visit or phone call and safety laboratory assessment should occur between Day 14 and Day 18. A phone call may take place on a different day than the blood draw within the Day 14 through Day 18 period.
g. If needed to confirm menopause.
1. Parameters include age and sex. Race and ethnicity will be collected where permitted by local regulations.
2. Wilson disease history will include diagnosis date, method of diagnosis, history of cirrhosis, details of any previous liver biopsies performed, and treatment received.

3. A full physical examination will be performed at Screening, at check_in for the study, and at the End of the study/Early Termination Visit. A physical examination should also be performed on any participants with ongoing adverse events prior to discharge from the unit. Otherwise, a symptom_driven physical examination may be performed at other times, at the Principal Investigator’s discretion.
4. Height at screening only.

5. While in the CRU, study intervention will be administered after an overnight fast (or, at least 10 hours) at the same time every morning; drug is to be administered with 240 mL of water and meals should be delayed a minimum of 2 hours after dosing. As an outpatient, participants are expected to take ALXN1840 at approximately the same time daily (+/_1 hour). Participants should take the medication with a glass of water on an empty stomach; meals should be delayed a minimum of 2 hours after dosing.
6. During the outpatient period, participants will use SMS text messaging to confirm study intervention administration.
7. Blood sampling for PK/PD will occur before ALXN1840 administration and represents a predose trough. See Table 2 for PK/PD sampling on Days 1, 25, 29, and 39.
8. PK/PD collection will include timepoints described in the schedule of PK/PD assessment for Days 1, 25, 29, and 39 (Table 2).

9. Samples for serum chemistry will be obtained following a fast of at least 8 hours at the Screening Visit and at check_in. In case of rechecks, and postdose serum chemistry, participants may not have fasted for 6 or 8 hours before the serum chemistry sample is taken.
10. Laboratory assessment including chemistry, hematology, and coagulation parameters should be performed on Days _8, _1, 8, 23, and 28 only.

11. Menstruation check is for women only; women of childbearing potential should only be enrolled after a negative serum pregnancy test result at Screening. Additional urine pregnancy testing will be standard for the protocol unless serum testing is required by site policies, local regulation, or Institutional Review Boards (IRBs)/Independent Ethics Committees (IECs) (Section 10.2). In addition to pregnancy tests detailed at the visits in the SoA, females of childbearing potential will be required to perform urine pregnancy tests at least every 4 weeks at their home or the study site throughout their time in the study.
12. A single 15 mL serum sample will be retained for evaluation in the event of an unexpected safety finding; retained samples may be destroyed after completion of the clinical study report.

13. Vital signs assessments and ECGs should be performed predose, unless otherwise specified. Vitals signs include body temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, and systolic and diastolic blood pressure.

14. Copper_ and molybdenum_controlled diet will be initiated after registration/inclusion. Participants will remain on a copper/molybdenum controlled diet throughout both the Inpatient Period 1 (Day 8 to Day 9) and Inpatient Period 2 (Day 23 to Day 40); during this time participants will be strongly encouraged to complete all meals. While not in the CRU, participants will be encouraged to adhere to their usual copper_controlled diet.
15. For each input/output balance period, 24_hour urine samples are to be collected for measurement of copper and molybdenum.
16. For fecal samples, each individual sample will be collected at each meal; the mean daily copper:molybdenum ratio in urine and feces at steady state compared with predose baseline.

data.text: This amendment is considered to be substantial based on the criteria set forth in Article 10(a) of Directive 2001/20/EC of the European Parliament and the Council of the European Union, the US Food and Drug Administration’s (FDA) regulation at 21 CFR part 312.30(b), and any applicable local regulations.

The main reason for preparation of this amendment was to clarify study procedures occurring across different sites in the US, so as to facilitate participant recruitment across different regions of the US, and to lessen inconvenience for participants without compromising the quality of the study.

data.name: Age requirement
data.text: Participants aged ≥18 at the time of signing the ICF.

data.name: Diagnosis of WD
data.text: Diagnosis of WD by Leipzig Criteria ≥ 4 documented by testing as outlined in the 2012 European Association for the Study of Liver WD Clinical Practice Guidelines (Ferenci, 2003; EASL, 2012) or by historical test results for WD including some or all of the following: • Presence of Kayser Fleischer rings, • Neurological symptoms, • Serum ceruloplasmin below reference range, • Coombs_negative hemolytic anemia, • Elevated liver or urinary copper, • Presence of mutations in the ATP7B gene, or • Other, as considered appropriate, may be used instead to confirm the diagnosis of WD.

data.name: Adherence to controlled diet
data.text: Participants willing to adhere to copper/molybdenum_controlled diet during inpatient periods and willing to comply with a low copper dietary requirement during the Outpatient Period.

data.name: Contraception requirements
data.text: Contraceptive use by men or women should be consistent with local regulations regarding the methods of contraception for those participating in clinical studies. a. Male participants: • Male participants, if heterosexually active and with a female spouse or partner of childbearing potential or a pregnant or breastfeeding spouse or partner, must agree to use barrier contraception (male condom) for the duration of the study and for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. Male participants must not donate sperm for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. • Female spouses or partners of male participants who are of childbearing potential must use highly effective contraception as defined below and in Section 10.4, starting at least 1 menstrual cycle before (the male participant’s) first study intervention administration and continuing until at least 3 months after the end of their male partner’s systemic exposure to the study intervention. • Barrier contraception (male condom) is required even with documented medical assessment of surgical success of a vasectomy. For male participants who have had a vasectomy (with documented evidence of azoospermia if possible) and agree to use a male condom for the stated time period, no additional contraceptive method is required by their female partner. b. Female participants: • Female participants or female partners of male participants of childbearing potential (including breastfeeding females), if heterosexually active, must be willing to follow protocol_specified contraception guidance starting at least 1 menstrual cycle before first study intervention administration and continuing for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. Female participants must not donate ova for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. • Female participants who are documented as being of non_childbearing potential as defined in Section 10.4 are exempt from contraception requirements. • Highly effective contraceptive methods for female participants and female partners of male participants are described in Section 10.4.

data.name: Informed consent
data.text: Participants capable of giving signed informed consent, which includes compliance with the requirements and restrictions listed in the ICF and in this protocol.

data.name: Decompensated cirrhosis or high MELD score
data.text: Decompensated cirrhosis or model for end_stage liver disease (MELD) score > 13.

data.name: High Modified Nazer score
data.text: Modified Nazer score > 7 (Dhawan, 2005).

data.name: Recent gastrointestinal bleed
data.text: Clinically significant gastrointestinal bleed within past 3 months.

data.name: Elevated ALT
data.text: Alanine aminotransferase (ALT) > 2 × upper limit of normal (ULN).

data.name: Marked neurological disease
data.text: Marked neurological disease requiring assistance with self_care or activities of daily life.

data.name: Low hemoglobin
data.text: Hemoglobin less than lower limit of the reference range for age and sex.

data.name: Active viral infections
data.text: Active infection with hepatitis B virus (positive hepatitis B surface antigen) or C virus (participants with positive hepatitis C antibody result would require confirmation of active disease with a positive hepatitis C polymerase chain reaction test), or seropositivity for human immunodeficiency virus (HIV).

data.name: Significant medical or psychiatric disorders
data.text: History or presence of/significant history of or current cardiovascular, respiratory, hepatic, renal, gastrointestinal, endocrinological, hematological, or neurological disorders, or psychiatric disorder that in the opinion of the Investigator may constitute a risk when taking the study intervention; or may interfere with the interpretation of data.

data.name: Previous treatment with ALXN1840 or tetrathiomolybdate
data.text: Previous treatment with ALXN1840 or other form of tetrathiomolybdate within 1 year prior to dosing.

data.name: Previous treatment with zinc
data.text: Previous treatment with zinc within 21 days prior to Day 1.

data.name: Use of experimental or unapproved therapy
data.text: The use of an experimental or unapproved/unlicensed therapy at the same time or within 90 days or 5 half_lives, whichever is longer, prior to the Screening Visit.

data.name: Renal failure
data.text: Participants in renal failure, defined as in end_stage renal disease on dialysis (chronic kidney disease [CKD] stage 5) or creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min (Levey, 2006).

data.name: Pregnancy or breastfeeding
data.text: Pregnant (or females who are planning to become pregnant) or breastfeeding females (women of childbearing potential must have a negative serum pregnancy test result at screening).

data.name: Known sensitivity to study intervention
data.text: Known sensitivity to ALXN1840, ALXN1840 excipients (anhydrous dicalcium phosphate, anhydrous sodium carbonate), or any of the ingredients contained in ALXN1840 or related compounds.

data.name: Likely non_compliant or uncooperative
data.text: In the opinion of the Investigator, the participant and/or their legal guardian is likely to be non_compliant or uncooperative during the study.

data.name: History of substance or alcohol abuse
data.text: History of illicit drug abuse, history of significant alcohol abuse within 1 year prior to the Screening Visit, or clinical evidence of substance and/or alcohol abuse within the 2 years before screening. Alcohol abuse is defined as regular weekly intake of more than 14 units (for both males and females), using the following National Health Service (NHS) alcohol tracker http://www.nhs.uk/Tools/Pages/drinks_tracker.aspx.

data.name: Positive urine drug toxicology screen
data.text: Positive urine drug toxicology screen at Screening or on Day _8 (cannabinoids will not be tested).

data.name: Recent alcohol consumption or positive breath test
data.text: Alcohol consumption within 48 hours prior to study intervention administration or positive alcohol breath test at screening or on Day _8.

data.name: Unwillingness to complete meals or tolerate diet
data.text: Participants unwilling to consistently complete every meal and tolerate a controlled, limited menu for the duration of the study.

data.name: Primary Objective: Assess net copper balance
data.text: Assess net copper balance with daily repeat_dose ALXN1840 treatment (15 mg and 30 mg) in participants with Wilson disease (WD)

data.name: Secondary Objective: Assess change in copper balance
data.text: Assess change in copper balance in response to ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day during ALXN1840 accumulation and at steady state periods versus the pretreatment baseline in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Investigate effect on copper disposition
data.text: Investigate the effect of ALXN1840 (15 mg/day and 30 mg/day) on the disposition of copper in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Investigate effect on molybdenum disposition
data.text: Investigate the effect of ALXN1840 on the disposition of molybdenum at steady state at 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Assess steady_state total molybdenum balance
data.text: Assess steady_state total molybdenum balance as a measure of ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Assess accumulation of molybdenum
data.text: Assess accumulation of molybdenum with ALXN1840 treatment at 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Determine steady_state plasma PK of molybdenum
data.text: Determine the steady_state plasma pharmacokinetic (PK) of total molybdenum and plasma ultrafiltrate (PUF) molybdenum (as surrogate measures of ALXN1840, 15 mg and 30 mg) in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Evaluate safety and tolerability
data.text: Evaluate the safety and tolerability of repeated_dose administration of ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day in participants with WD

data.name: Exploratory Objective: Determine dose response for copper balance
data.text: Determine dose response of ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day for copper balance in participants with WD

data.name: Exploratory Objective: Determine effect of treatment duration on copper balance
data.text: Determine the effect of treatment duration on copper balance in participants with WD

data.name: Exploratory Objective: Assess effects on ceruloplasmin, ceruloplasmin_bound copper LBC profiles
data.text: Assess the effects of ALXN1840 on ceruloplasmin, ceruloplasmin_bound copper LBC profiles in plasma in participants with WD

data.name: Primary Endpoint: Mean daily copper balance
data.text: Mean daily copper balance where copper balance is measured by the calculated difference between copper intake (in food and drink) and copper output (in feces and urine) during ALXN1840 accumulation and steady_state periods for each dose

data.name: Secondary Endpoint: Change in mean daily copper balance
data.text: Change in mean daily copper balance as measured by the calculated difference between copper intake (in food and drink) and copper output (in feces and urine) from pretreatment baseline (Days _4 through _1) and ALXN1840 accumulation and steady_state periods for each dose

data.name: Secondary Endpoint: Copper quantified in food, drink, feces, urine, plasma total and labile bound copper (LBC)
data.text: Copper quantified in food, drink, feces, and urine, including plasma total and labile bound copper (LBC) during ALXN1840 accumulation and steady_state periods for each dose

data.name: Secondary Endpoint: Molybdenum quantified in food, drink, feces, urine, plasma total molybdenum
data.text: Molybdenum quantified in food, drink, feces, and urine, plasma total molybdenum at ALXN1840 steady state

data.name: Secondary Endpoint: Mean daily molybdenum balance
data.text: Mean daily molybdenum balance as demonstrated through measurement of molybdenum intake (in food, drink and ALXN1840), and molybdenum output (feces and urine) representing ALXN1840 steady state

data.name: Secondary Endpoint: Accumulation of molybdenum
data.text: Accumulation of molybdenum as determined by molybdenum balance

data.name: Secondary Endpoint: PK parameters for plasma total and PUF_molybdenum

data.name: Amendment 1
data.summary: The main reason for preparation of this amendment was to update procedures outlined in the Schedule of Activities, remove contradictory text on the reporting of serious adverse events, and add details of an interim analysis. Additional, minor changes are included in the table below.

data.name: Amendment 2
data.summary: The main reason for preparation of this amendment was to revise the exclusion criterion for a urine drug screen. Changes implemented via Administrative Letter 1 and Administrative Letter 2, as well as COVID vaccination guidance, have also been incorporated.

data.name: Amendment 3
data.summary: The main reason for preparation of this amendment was to update the washout period for zinc. Additional changes are listed below.

data.name: Amendment 3.1 (US)
data.summary: The main reason for preparation of this amendment was to clarify study procedures occurring across different sites in the US, so as to facilitate participant recruitment across different regions of the US, and to lessen inconvenience for participants without compromising the quality of the study.

data.name: ALXN1840
data.description: Novel copper binding agent for Wilson disease, containing bis_choline tetrathiomolybdate.

data.name: Copper_chelating therapies
data.description: Must be discontinued for approximately 4 days prior to ALXN1840 initiation. Examples include penicillamine or trientine.

data.name: Zinc therapy
data.description: Must be discontinued for a minimum of 21 days prior to Day 1.

data.name: Previous ALXN1840 or other form of tetrathiomolybdate
data.description: Must be discontinued within 1 year prior to dosing.

data.name: Experimental or unapproved/unlicensed therapy
data.description: Must be discontinued within 90 days or 5 half_lives (whichever is longer) prior to the Screening Visit.

data.name: Prescription medications (general)
data.description: Must abstain from taking within 14 days or 5 half_lives (whichever is longer) of Day _7, except as permitted.

data.name: Nonprescription drugs (including vitamins and dietary or herbal supplements)
data.description: Must abstain from taking within 7 days or 5 half_lives (whichever is longer) before Day _7, except as permitted.

data.name: Herbal remedies
data.description: Must be discontinued at least 7 days prior to first admission until study completion.

data.name: St John’s wort
data.description: Must be discontinued 2 weeks before the planned first study intervention administration.

data.name: Minerals containing copper, zinc, or molybdenum
data.description: Participants must avoid use throughout the study duration.

data.name: Copper/molybdenum_controlled diet
data.description: Initiated on Day _7 and continued throughout inpatient periods; low copper dietary requirement encouraged during outpatient period.

data.name: Paracetamol/acetaminophen
data.description: Permitted at doses of a maximum 1000 mg per day, with Investigator approval.

data.name: Ibuprofen
data.description: Permitted at doses not exceeding 1200 mg in any 24_hour period, with Investigator approval.

data.name: bis_choline tetrathiomolybdate
data.description: Active pharmaceutical ingredient of ALXN1840, formerly WTX101.

data.name: tetrathiomolybdic acid
data.description: Component of ALXN1840, 7.8 mg per 15 mg tablet.

data.name: tetrathiomolybdate
data.description: Active anion in ALXN1840, a copper binding agent.

SRC Safety Review Before Dose Titration
data.description: Safety Review Committee reviews available safety data through Day 23 for each participant before titration to 30 mg/day
data.text: Before titration to 30 mg/day, the Safety Review Committee (SRC) will review available safety data through Day 23 for each participant.

data.name: ALT Elevation > 5x ULN
data.description: ALT elevation greater than 5 times the upper limit of normal triggers dose modification
data.text: ALT > 5 × ULN: Temporary interruption. Weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent, when ALT is ≤ 3 × ULN.

data.name: ALT Elevation > 3x ULN
data.description: ALT elevation greater than 3 times ULN triggers dose reduction or restriction
data.text: ALT > 3 × ULN: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up_titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution of abnormality.

data.name: Hemoglobin or Platelet Decrease > 30% from Baseline
data.description: Greater than 30% decrease from baseline in hemoglobin or platelets triggers dose modification
data.text: Hemoglobin or Platelets > 30% decrease from baseline: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up_titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution of abnormality.

data.name: Neutrophil Decrease > 30% from Baseline with Below_Range Baseline
data.description: Greater than 30% decrease from baseline in neutrophils when neutrophils are below reference range at baseline
data.text: Neutrophils > 30% decrease from baseline with neutrophils below reference range at baseline: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up_titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution.

data.name: Bilirubin > 2x ULN with ALT > 3x ULN
data.description: Bilirubin greater than 2 times ULN accompanied by ALT greater than 3 times ULN indicative of liver injury
data.text: Bilirubin > 2 × ULN accompanied by ALT > 3 × ULN, indicative of liver injury: Temporary interruption. Weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent, when bilirubin is below ULN. Rechallenge requires approval of the Alexion Medical Monitor.

data.name: Neurological Worsening
data.description: Evidence of neurologic worsening by AEs or by neurologic physical exam assessment
data.text: Evidence of neurologic worsening by AEs or by neurologic physical exam assessment: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data.

data.name: Psychiatric Worsening
data.description: Evidence of clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening including suicidality, acute depression, or psychosis
data.text: Evidence of clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening which may include but not limited to suicidality, acute depression, or psychosis: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification.

data.name: Serious Hypersensitivity Reaction
data.description: Occurrence of serious hypersensitivity reaction requires discontinuation consideration
data.text: Serious hypersensitivity reaction

data.name: Severe Uncontrolled Infection
data.description: Occurrence of severe uncontrolled infection requires discontinuation consideration
data.text: Severe uncontrolled infection

data.name: Use of Disallowed Medication
data.description: Use of disallowed medication requires discontinuation consideration
data.text: Use of disallowed medication

data.name: Pregnancy or Planned Pregnancy
data.description: Pregnancy or planned pregnancy requires discontinuation consideration
data.text: Pregnancy or planned pregnancy

data.name: Clinically Significant QTcF Change
data.description: Clinically significant change from baseline in QTcF after enrollment
data.text: If a clinically significant finding is identified (including, but not limited to changes from baseline in QT interval corrected using Fridericia's formula [QTcF]) after enrollment, the Investigator or qualified designee will determine if the participant can continue in the study.

data.name: Safety Concern Preventing Dose Titration
data.description: Safety concerns that may place participants at undue risk prevent titration to 30 mg/day
data.text: Unless there are safety concerns that, in the opinion of the Investigator(s) and/or Alexion, may place participants at undue risk, in which case, participants may remain on the 15 mg/day dose for the duration of the study.

data.name: Maximum Rechallenge Limit
data.description: A maximum of 3 rechallenges will be allowed for dose modifications
data.text: A maximum of 3 rechallenges will be allowed.

data.name: Enrollment to Run_in Period
data.text: Participants eligible for the study will be enrolled on Day _7 and initiated on a copper_ and molybdenum_controlled diet. Participants will be admitted to the CRU on Day _8.

data.name: Run_in to Treatment Period 1 (15 mg/day)
data.text: Following the Run_in Period, participants will be administered ALXN1840 at 15 mg/day starting Day 1 for approximately 28 days.

data.name: Treatment Period 1 to Outpatient Period
data.text: Participants may be discharged from the CRU on Day 9 to return on Day 22 or Day 23 (predose). The Outpatient Period may be extended up to an additional 14 days with Investigator approval.

data.name: Outpatient Period to Inpatient Period 2
data.text: Participants who are discharged will be re_admitted on Day 22 or Day 23 for Treatment Period 2 and will remain in house until Day 40. All procedures will start on Day 23.

data.name: Dose Titration from 15 mg to 30 mg
data.text: Participants will be administered ALXN1840 at a dose of 15 mg/day on Day 1 through Day 28 and then increase to 30 mg/day on Day 29 through Day 39. Before titration to 30 mg/day, the SRC will review available safety data through Day 23 for each participant.

data.name: Dose Reduction for ALT > 3x ULN
data.text: ALT > 3 × ULN: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up_titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution of abnormality. Weekly repeat testing.

data.name: Temporary Interruption for ALT > 5x ULN
data.text: ALT > 5 × ULN: Temporary interruption. Weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent, when ALT is ≤ 3 × ULN.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Hemoglobin/Platelet Decrease > 30%
data.text: Hemoglobin or Platelets > 30% decrease from baseline: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up_titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution.

data.name: Temporary Interruption for Bilirubin > 2x ULN with ALT > 3x ULN
data.text: Bilirubin > 2 × ULN accompanied by ALT > 3 × ULN: Temporary interruption. Weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent, when bilirubin is below ULN. Requires approval of Alexion Medical Monitor.

data.name: Dose Modification for Neurological Worsening
data.text: Evidence of neurologic worsening: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data.

data.name: Dose Modification for Psychiatric Worsening
data.text: Evidence of clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data.

data.name: Discontinuation for Serious Hypersensitivity
data.text: Serious hypersensitivity reaction requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Severe Uncontrolled Infection
data.text: Severe uncontrolled infection requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Disallowed Medication Use
data.text: Use of disallowed medication requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Pregnancy
data.text: Pregnancy or planned pregnancy requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Abnormal Liver Function
data.text: Discontinuation of study intervention for abnormal liver function should be considered by the Investigator when a participant meets one of the conditions outlined in the Dose Modification criteria (Section 6.6) or if the Investigator believes that it is in best interest of the participant.

data.name: Treatment Completion to Post_Study Therapy
data.text: Following completion of both inpatient periods of the study (ie, Day 40), participants will either transition to therapy that was discontinued before enrollment, transition to other standard of care therapy, or consider continuation of ALXN1840 treatment.

data.name: Remain on 15 mg Due to Safety Concerns
data.text: Unless there are safety concerns that, in the opinion of the Investigator(s) and/or Alexion, may place participants at undue risk, in which case, participants may remain on the 15 mg/day dose for the duration of the study.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Neutrophil Decrease
data.text: Neutrophils > 30% decrease from baseline with neutrophils below reference range at baseline: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up_titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD.

data.name: Participant Voluntary Withdrawal
data.description: A participant may withdraw from the study at any time at his/her own request.

data.name: Investigator_Initiated Withdrawal
data.description: A participant may be withdrawn at any time at the discretion of the Investigator for safety, behavioral, compliance, or administrative reasons.

data.name: Study Completion
data.description: A participant is considered to have completed the study if he/she has completed all phases of the study including the last scheduled procedure shown in the Schedule of Activities. End of Study Visit on Day 54 (+/_ 2 days).

data.name: Lost to Follow_up
data.description: A participant will be considered lost to follow_up if he or she repeatedly fails to return for scheduled visits and is unable to be contacted by the study site. At least 3 telephone calls and a certified letter must be attempted before deeming lost to follow_up.

data.name: Study Intervention Discontinuation _ Remains in Study
data.description: If the study intervention is definitively discontinued, the participant should remain in the study to be evaluated for safety follow_up.

data.name: Alexion Study Termination
data.description: Alexion reserves the right to close the study site or terminate the study at any time for any reason at the sole discretion of Alexion.

data.name: Investigator_Initiated Site Closure
data.description: The Investigator may initiate study_site closure at any time, provided there is reasonable cause and sufficient notice is given in advance of the intended termination.

data.name: SRC Safety Review Before Dose Titration
data.description: Before titration to 30 mg/day on Day 29, the Safety Review Committee will review available safety data through Day 23 for each participant to determine if dose escalation is appropriate.

data.name: ALT_Based Dose Modification Decision
data.description: At any safety assessment, ALT levels are evaluated. ALT > 3x ULN triggers dose reduction; ALT > 5x ULN triggers temporary interruption with rechallenge when ALT ≤ 3x ULN.

data.name: Hematology_Based Dose Modification Decision
data.description: At any safety assessment, hemoglobin, platelets, and neutrophils are evaluated. Greater than 30% decrease from baseline triggers dose reduction.

data.name: Liver Injury Assessment Decision
data.description: Bilirubin > 2x ULN accompanied by ALT > 3x ULN triggers temporary interruption. Rechallenge requires Alexion Medical Monitor approval.

data.name: Neurological/Psychiatric Assessment Decision
data.description: Evidence of neurologic or psychiatric worsening requires evaluation by Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor for dose modification based on copper control parameters and clinical data.

data.name: QTcF Assessment Decision
data.description: If clinically significant QTcF change is identified after enrollment, the Investigator determines if the participant can continue and if any change in management is needed.

data.name: Outpatient Period Extension Decision
data.description: At Day 9, the Investigator may approve extension of the Outpatient Period up to an additional 14 days. Participants will be given additional investigational product for daily dosing.

data.name: CRU Retention During Outpatient Period Decision
data.description: At the CRU's discretion, participants may remain in the CRU during the outpatient period for safety or to maintain the integrity of the conduct of the study.

data.name: Discontinuation Assessment Decision
data.description: At any time during the study, participants must be considered for discontinuation if serious hypersensitivity, severe uncontrolled infection, disallowed medication use, or pregnancy occurs.
